patch.value: Repeated loose-mapped --tag flags silently keep only the last value (agent-unfriendly)

Dogfood 2026-05-25: 'pm create issue X --tag a --tag b --tag c' silently persists only the LAST tag (c). Root cause: the canonical flag is --tags (single comma-separated value); --tag is accepted via the loose-option parser (main.ts allowUnknownOption(true) + normalization) and mapped onto the scalar tags option, so repeated occurrences overwrite instead of accumulating. No warning is emitted. Agents commonly use the repeatable-flag convention (--tag a --tag b) seen in many CLIs, so they end up with silent data loss. This is DISTINCT from pm-klqo (--tags JSON-array corruption, closed) and pm-swie (--test key corruption, closed): those fixed parseTags/parseTests value parsing; this is about the loose-option mapping accumulating repeated occurrences of comma-list flags. Fix proposal: when the loose-option layer maps a repeated near-miss of a comma-list option (tags/skills/blockedBy/etc.), accumulate the union of values rather than overwriting; alternatively emit a one-line warning on silent overwrite. Acceptance: 'pm create X --tag a --tag b' yields tags [a,b]; documented '--tags a,b' unchanged; regression tests cover repeated singular + plural mix. Low/med severity (documented --tags a,b,c works).

value.author: claude-code-agent
value.text: Execution start 2026-05-25 (claude-code-agent orchestrator): reproduced data loss in sandbox — 'pm create issue X --tag alpha --tag beta --tag gamma' persists only tags=[gamma]. Root cause confirmed: normalizeBootstrapInvocation (src/cli/bootstrap-args.ts) rewrites each '--tag V' to canonical '--tags V' via levenshtein typo match, then Commander's scalar --tags option keeps only the LAST occurrence. Same keep-last class affects genuine repeats of comma-list flags (--tags a --tags b) and list-filter comma flags (pm list --tag a --tag b, --status, --type, --priority, --fields). Fix lane: mark comma-list flags in CliFlagContract and coalesce repeated canonical list-flag occurrences into one comma-joined token at normalization time. Branch fix/agent-ux-repeated-list-flags-accumulate.

author: claude-code-flag-engineer
message: Link files changed for repeated list-flag accumulation fix (pm-cf1u)

author: claude-code-flag-engineer
message: Link files changed for repeated list-flag accumulation fix (pm-cf1u)

value.author: claude-code-flag-engineer
value.text:
Implemented argv-level coalescing of repeated comma-separated list flags (fixes the keep-last data loss).

Step 1 (cli-contracts.ts): added optional list?: boolean to CliFlagContract and marked 9 flags list:true -- CREATE --tags, UPDATE --tags, UPDATE_MANY --tags, PLAN --tags + PLAN --fields, LIST_FILTER --status + --fields, GET --fields, SEARCH --fields. Deliberately did NOT mark list.ts scalar filters --tag/--type/--priority (verified list.ts parses --tag as scalar), nor any repeatable:true seed flag (--dep/--comment use Commander variadic collector).

Step 2 (bootstrap-args.ts): added listCanonicalFlags:Set to FlagLookup (populated in buildFlagLookup using the same canonical formula as canonicalFlag), added list_merge to the reason union, and a pure exported coalesceRepeatedListFlags(argv,listFlags) that walks argv L->R, merges repeats of a canonical list flag into the FIRST occurrence as one --flag=v1,v2,v3 token, drops later flag+value tokens, handles both '--flag value' and '--flag=value' forms, stops at --, and leaves value-less occurrences untouched. Wired it after the per-token loop in normalizeBootstrapInvocation; events pushed into trace.

DESIGN DECISION: coalesce at argv-normalization time rather than switching these flags to Commander variadic collectors. Rationale: (a) the root cause is normalizeBootstrapInvocation rewriting --tag->--tags per-occurrence, so the singular-typo case must be handled in this layer anyway; (b) downstream parsers (parseTags, list/get/search field+status) already split a single comma string, so a merged token flows through unchanged with zero downstream edits; (c) Commander variadic would change option types/contracts/MCP schemas broadly and not fix the singular --tag typo path; (d) single-occurrence inputs stay byte-identical (no = rewrite), preserving existing behavior.

VERIFICATION: pnpm build clean. Targeted tests: 52 passed (bootstrap-args.spec.ts + new list-flag-accumulation.integration.spec.ts). Manual proof in isolated sandbox: create issue X --tag a --tag b --tag c -> get item.tags=[a,b,c]; create issue Y --tags p --tags q -> get item.tags=[p,q].

value.author: claude-code-flag-engineer
value.text:
Full gate results (all green):

- node scripts/run-tests.mjs coverage: 121 test files, 1797 tests passed. Coverage 100% statements (2493/2493), 100% branches (1810/1810), 100% functions (463/463), 100% lines (2400/2400).
- pnpm quality:static: Static quality gate passed. (changed files within limits: cli-contracts.ts 1818 LOC, bootstrap-args.ts 678, bootstrap-args.spec.ts 381, list-flag-accumulation.integration.spec.ts 39; left cli.integration.spec.ts at 6989 untouched to avoid crossing the 7000 test-line gate, hence the new sibling integration spec.)
- node scripts/check-secrets.mjs: No credential-like secrets detected in tracked files.

Tests added: 13 unit cases for normalizeBootstrapInvocation (repeated singular typo, repeated plural, list-filter --status, repeated --fields, mixed space+inline, single occurrence unchanged, non-list --title not merged, -- terminator) and coalesceRepeatedListFlags (empty set passthrough, multi-flag independent merge, order preservation, value-less trailing flag, following-flag-not-a-value, -- remainder verbatim), plus a list_merge trace-event shape assertion ('--tags (x3)' -> ['--tags=a,b,c']). 2 integration regression cases run the real built dist.

No deviations from spec except: integration regression placed in a new sibling spec file tests/integration/list-flag-accumulation.integration.spec.ts (not appended to cli.integration.spec.ts) because that file is already 6989 lines and the static gate test limit is 7000 -- adding to it would risk breaking the gate. No new src files were added (coverage-include allowlist untouched). package.json version unchanged; item left in_progress for review.

value.author: claude-code-agent
value.text: Orchestrator finalization (claude-code-agent): (1) Added --group-by (aggregate) to the list:true set after verifying parseGroupBy splits commas and repeated --group-by previously kept-last; sandbox confirms 'aggregate --group-by status --group-by type' now matches the comma form. (2) Independent regression: dedicated dogfood sub-agent ran a 28/28 PASS full-workflow sweep on the rebuilt dist (init/create all types/list/get/claim/update/close/restore/comments/notes/learnings/files/docs/context/search/contracts/aggregate/stats/history --verify/validate/health/plan/deps/schema) with ZERO breakage; history --verify ok, validate ok, health ok. (3) Final gates green: node scripts/run-tests.mjs coverage = 121 files / 1797 tests / 100% statements,branches,functions,lines; pnpm quality:static passed; check-secrets clean. (4) Dogfood surfaced two NEW on-theme follow-ups (no duplicates, dedup evidence recorded): pm-ch59 (create/update changed_fields output token bloat — deferred: 66 changed_fields test refs + create-vs-update design call warrants its own PR) and pm-8nyc (unknown-option did-you-mean / cross-command flag confusion — enhancement). Branch fix/agent-ux-repeated-list-flags-accumulate.

patch.value: Fixed: repeated comma-separated list flags now accumulate via argv-level coalescing (coalesceRepeatedListFlags in src/cli/bootstrap-args.ts) gated by a new CliFlagContract 'list: true' marker. Covers --tags (create/update/update-many/plan), --fields (list/get/search/plan), --status (list), and --group-by (aggregate).
Shipped argv-level coalescing of repeated comma-separated list flags; verified by full coverage gate (1797 tests/100%) and a 28/28 dogfood regression sweep.
pm create issue X --tag a --tag b --tag c yields tags [a,b,c]; repeated --tags/--status/--fields/--group-by accumulate identically to the comma form.
Before fix: only the last value was kept (tags=[gamma]) due to Commander scalar keep-last after --tag was typo-normalized to --tags. After fix: all values accumulate via coalesceRepeatedListFlags.

value.author: claude-code-agent
value.text: PR #70 review follow-up (Codex P2): coalesceRepeatedListFlags greedily consumed the token after a list-flag as its value, which could misread a list-flag-looking token that is actually the value of a value-consuming option (notably global --path, which accepts values beginning with --). Repro before fix: 'pm --path --tags create issue X --tags a --tags b' swallowed the 'create' command token -> 'Unknown command issue'. Fix: coalesce now skips the value token of value-consuming flags (GLOBAL_VALUE_CONSUMING_FLAGS={--path}; other globals are boolean) so a --prefixed value is never reinterpreted as a flag nor swallows the following command/positional. Added regression unit test. Verified: '--path --tags create issue X --tags a --tags b' now parses create + tags=[a,b] (path='--tags' -> correct 'Tracker not initialized'); normal repeated --tag still accumulates. Gates green: 121 files/1798 tests/100% coverage, static quality, secrets clean. CodeRabbit/Sourcery rate-limited (no findings); Gemini: no issues.
